name: ALMA CCI Mean-Reversion Recovery
edge_hypothesis: ALMA_9_6.0_0.85 (Arnaud Legoux Moving Average) has NEVER been tested as a strategy baseline despite being available since the indicator library was built. Its Gaussian-weighted kernel with offset=6.0 and sigma=0.85 concentrates weight ahead of the current bar, giving it predictive responsiveness that standard EMA lacks — it should produce fewer false crossovers in choppy markets. CCI_20 crossing above -100 means price was more than 1 standard deviation below its 20-bar statistical mean and is now recovering — a mathematically rigorous oversold recovery signal. CCI-as-confirmation is validated (Supertrend CCI v4 ACCEPT, PF=1.290). The thesis: ALMA's Gaussian smoothing identifies trend direction with less lag than EMA, while CCI recovery from statistical extreme times the exact pullback completion. Two underused indicators that address different aspects of the entry problem — direction (ALMA) and timing (CCI). Ranging regimes should benefit most since CCI oscillation is pronounced when price fluctuates around its mean.

variants.name: alma_cci_recovery_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core variant. Price above ALMA Gaussian mean + CCI recovering from statistically oversold (-100). 8:1 R:R sweet spot, 1.5 ATR stop.

components.notes: Gaussian-weighted adaptive average — FIRST EVER ALMA baseline test. Offset 6.0 + sigma 0.85 provides forward-looking smoothing.
CCI crossing above -100 = recovery from >1 std dev below statistical mean. Validated pattern (Supertrend CCI v4 ACCEPT).
ATR-based position sizing and 8:1 R:R framework.

variants.name: alma_cci_recovery_v2_10to1
variants.description: Extended TP variant. Tests whether ALMA's superior trend identification supports longer holds. 10:1 R:R (15x ATR TP). If ALMA filters noise better than EMA, entries should be cleaner with more runway for extended targets.

components.notes: CCI statistical extreme recovery — wider TP tests extended hold thesis.
Extended 15x ATR TP for trend continuation.

variants.name: alma_cci_recovery_v3_relaxed_8to1
variants.description: Relaxed CCI threshold variant. CCI crosses above -80 instead of -100 — captures more moderate pullbacks (not just extreme oversold). More trades expected but less extreme entry conditions. Tests whether ALMA's directional filter is strong enough to make moderate CCI readings profitable.

components.notes: Relaxed CCI -80 threshold — moderate pullback recovery, higher trade frequency.

advisory_context.novel_element: FIRST EVER ALMA baseline test + CCI statistical extreme recovery. Two underused indicators combined.

indicator_roles.baseline: ALMA_9_6.0_0.85 — Gaussian-weighted adaptive average, never tested as standalone baseline
indicator_roles.confirmation: CCI_20_0.015 — statistical extreme recovery timing, validated by Supertrend CCI v4 ACCEPT
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — position sizing and R:R framework
